data.description:
## Summary

The `external integration (live services)` job of the `external-integration` GitHub Action
fails at the pytest step. Every live LLM call raises immediately (0.1s, before any network
I/O): `Model requests are not allowed, since ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS is False`, surfacing as
`test_execution_mode_live.py:44 AssertionError: assert 'failed' == 'succeeded'`.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Trigger the `external-integration` workflow (or run `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1 pytest -m external tests/external`).
2. Observe the live tests fail with the `ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS is False` RuntimeError.

Expected: the external tier makes REAL model calls and the live tests pass.
Actual: the tier's model calls are blocked before any network I/O; the job fails.

## Root Cause

`tests/conftest.py::_no_live_model_requests` (session-scoped autouse, added 2026-06-29 in
commit 9450d0462 / PR #85) sets pydantic-ai's global `models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False`
as a CI safety net against accidental billing. The opt-in external tier never re-enables the
switch, so all its live calls raise.

Confirmed by guard-commit ancestry vs run outcome: last success (sha aa3ae84ab, 06-16) had
the guard ABSENT; both failing runs (07-06, 07-09) have it PRESENT.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The external tier re-enables `ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS` (only when opted in via `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL`), guarded for the lean lane where pydantic-ai is absent.
- [ ] The default suite's session safety-net remains intact (non-external tests still see the switch False).
- [ ] A RED->GREEN test in `tests/external/` proves the switch is on for the tier and makes no billable call.
- [ ] `make lint` passes on the change.
data.title: external-integration 'external' job fails: session test guard leaves ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS False for the live tier
